---
title: "3.3.3 Magnetic, optical and solid-state storage"
subtitle: "Memory sticks / flash memories · Hardware P125-P126"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.76em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.32em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)

## 3.3.3 Solid-state storage

### Memory sticks / flash memories

<span class="term">Memory sticks</span> / <span class="term">flash memories</span> (also known as <span class="term">pen drives</span>) use <span class="mark">solid state technology</span>.

They usually connect to the computer through the <span class="term">USB port</span>.

Their main advantage is that they are <span class="mark">very small, lightweight devices</span>, which make them very suitable as a method for <span class="mark">file transfer between computers</span>.

## Common Uses

### Transfer and back-up

Memory sticks / flash memories are <span class="term">non-volatile</span> devices and can also be used as <span class="mark">portable backup</span> devices for music or photo files, for example.

They are part of the <span class="term">solid-state storage</span> family, so they have <span class="mark">no moving parts</span>.

## Dongle Use

### Software protection

Complex or expensive software, such as financial planning software, often uses memory sticks as a <span class="term">software dongle</span> (软件加密狗).

The dongle contains <span class="mark">additional files</span> that are needed to run the software.

Without this dongle, the software will not work properly.

It therefore prevents <span class="mark">illegal or unauthorised use</span> of the software, and also prevents copying of the software since, without the dongle, it is useless.

## Classroom Check

### Keep the use cases specific

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- that memory sticks / flash memories use <span class="mark">solid-state technology</span>
- that they usually connect through the <span class="term">USB</span> port
- that they are <span class="mark">small and lightweight</span>, so they are useful for transferring files
- that they can be used as <span class="mark">small back-up devices</span>
- that as a <span class="term">dongle</span>, they contain additional files needed to run software
:::

## Bridge

### Next: optical storage

The next decks move from <span class="term">solid-state storage</span> to <span class="term">optical storage</span>.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)
